title: Staff search dashboard
card: Give employees one place to find approved procedures.
detail: Staff can search, browse by section, pin important SOPs, and open the current instruction set without digging through old folders or asking a manager.

title: QR codes for mobile SOP access
card: Put the right instruction at the workstation, shelf, or equipment area.
detail: QR links let staff scan from a phone or tablet and open the relevant SOP where the work is happening. It is built for office floors, service desks, equipment areas, and shared workstations.

title: AI-assisted SOP generator
card: Create a first draft from a business process description.
detail: Managers can use AI-assisted drafting to turn rough descriptions into structured procedure content, then review and approve the SOP before staff see it.

title: Import existing SOP content
card: Move current procedures into the portal instead of starting over.
detail: Existing documents can be converted into organized SOP content so the business can modernize the library without rewriting everything from scratch.

title: Checklist runs for repeatable work
card: Turn recurring work into trackable task runs.
detail: Checklist-style SOPs help staff complete repeatable work step by step, add notes, and leave a record that the task was performed.

title: Training questions and quiz builder
card: Build training checks into the procedure itself.
detail: Managers can add questions for key SOPs so onboarding and recurring training include confirmation, not just a document link.

title: Training completion records
card: See who completed training and where follow-up is needed.
detail: Training runs give managers a practical way to review completion history and confirm staff understanding of important procedures.

title: Staff change requests
card: Let staff report unclear, outdated, or missing instructions.
detail: Change requests keep the SOP library alive. Staff can flag improvements, while managers review and control what gets published.

title: Manager dashboard
card: Monitor drafts, requests, training, backups, and portal health.
detail: The manager dashboard centralizes administration so owners and managers can see what needs attention without hunting across separate tools.

title: Role-based SOP visibility
card: Show staff the SOPs relevant to their job.
detail: Role visibility helps keep the portal focused. Staff can access procedures that matter to their department or responsibilities.

title: Branded customer portal settings
card: Make the portal feel like part of the customer’s operation.
detail: Brand settings let the portal reflect the customer’s company name, portal name, colors, logo, and support details.

title: Backup and restore tools
card: Keep portal data backed up and recoverable.
detail: Backup and restore controls help protect the SOP library and support safer updates, testing, and customer maintenance workflows.
